The council wants to discuss alternative proposals and ensure that any changes are subject to the PEP process.
summary::Ask HN: Why is the HN crowd so: The Hacker News community is divided over the topic of AI, with some users expressing concerns about how artificial intelligence is being used to write bad code and introduce bugs in software products. This perception has led to derogatory comments from some users claiming that individuals who use AI are inexperienced or unintelligent. However, others argue that the issue is not as simple as it seems and that execution speed can take precedence over code elegance.
summary::No Let, No Rec, No Problem: A G: The Y and Z combinators are a pair of functions that enable functional programming by allowing you to solve simple problems without using loops or recursion. They provide a way to define recursive functions that don't call themselves directly, which is often necessary when dealing with certain types of computations. By avoiding explicit calls to the functions involved in the computation, developers can create more concise and efficient code.
summary::The back cover of C++: The Lang: The back cover of C++: The Programming Language raises questions not answered by the front cover, as it suggests a book about the language itself but does not provide any information on its contents or significance. This is in contrast to the front cover, which implies that the book will offer insights into the field of computer science and programming.
summary::S&#x26;P 500 rejects SpaceX, al: The S&P 500 stock market index has rejected SpaceX's request for swift entry into several leading indexes as a condition of its historic debut. This means the company will not gain accelerated access to billions more dollars through passive investment funds, which could have benefited from an exception allowing it to enter the index after its IPO. The decision is likely to be seen as a relief by those concerned about market risks and retirement savings plans exposed to SpaceX's speculative orbital data center plans.
summary::Did Claude increase bugs in rsy: The author of the article is investigating whether Claude increased bugs in the rsync package after he became involved with it. They are using AI assistance to analyze data and find the root cause of the issue, but they argue that this approach may not be reliable due to potential noise and inconsistencies in the data. The author spent several days rewriting their report to use a better methodology, which included writing the scripts themselves and templating numbers and statistics directly into the text.
summary::Ten Years of Franz: Franz 6 ships now, marking ten years since its launch in March 2016. The development of this desktop app has been marked by several key milestones, including a weekend prototype and a successful fundraising campaign that allowed it to gain traction on Product Hunt. Franz's founder notes that the success was largely due to the hard work and dedication put into developing the product over time, rather than just one initial launch.
summary::The perils of UUID primary keys: Random UUIDs can cause significant extra paging in clustered indexes, leading to poor performance. This is because the random nature of UUIDs means that rows are inserted into the B-tree in a seemingly random order, requiring re-balancing which can be time-consuming. The use of random UUIDs as primary keys can lead to wasted space and increased latency in databases like SQLite.
summary::Social Cache Busting: Public figures often use pre-prepared responses to their questions, which can make them seem more intelligent and in tune with what you've asked. However, these responses are typically based on a lookup table rather than genuine dialogue. This is not unique to public figures; most people engage in similar behavior when interacting with others. The degree of cache busting often correlates with how frequently individuals get asked the same questions. To effectively bust the cache, it's essential to notice that you're hitting it in the first place and be aware of your own motivations for asking certain questions.
summary::Magecart skimmer turns Stripe i: Magecart skimmers turn Stripe into malware command servers by storing stolen card information in the platform's customer metadata and using it to create fake customers on checkout pages, allowing attackers to exfiltrate sensitive data through two trusted domains: Google Tag Manager and Stripe. This technique exploits vulnerabilities in Content Security Policy rules and network filters that allow the skimmer to bypass security measures and upload stolen cards to an attacker's account.
summary::GrapheneOS user reported to aut: GrapheneOS user reported to authorities for using GrapheneOS, with Yoti flagging the device due to its automatic reporting of instances to both authorities and security teams. This highlights the issue with age verification companies like Yoti that prioritize security and privacy concerns over legitimate uses of technology. The incident raises questions about the balance between state and private interests in regulating emerging technologies.
summary::Zig Zen Update: The latest update for Zig Zen has introduced a feature that automatically detects and redirects malicious bot traffic. This is done through an automated check that verifies the user's JavaScript environment before allowing access to the site. The goal of this measure is to prevent unauthorized access and ensure a more secure browsing experience for users. By implementing such a system, developers can help protect their applications from potential security threats.
summary::AI Worm: Artificial intelligence agents have been found to enable adaptive computer worms that can exploit common corporate network vulnerabilities and propagate without human operators, creating a destabilizing economic asymmetry between attackers and defenders. These autonomous generative adversaries can reason about targets, adapt to observations, and synthesize attack logic in real-time, making them no longer theoretical threats.
summary::The Smart TV in Your LivingRoom: The work at Include Security has been collaborating with AI companies to use datacenters and training AI models, but there's another way to train these models - by scraping data from the internet using residential proxy networks. Bright Data is a company that sells access to its massive residential proxy network of 400 million home IP addresses, which can be used as exit nodes for web-scraping traffic. This means that smart TVs and other connected devices can be used as proxies to train AI models on scraped data from the internet.
summary::Google will pay SpaceX $920M pe: Google will pay SpaceX $920 million per month for access to approximately 110,000 NVIDIA GPUs, CPUs, memory, and other related components from October 2026 through June 2029. This deal is similar in length and scope to one with Anthropic, which paid SpaceX $1.25 billion per month for compute. Google's access to the data center will ramp up over a period of nine months before reaching its full capacity by September 30, 2026.
summary::Introduction – Rust for Python: Rust provides a comprehensive guide to learning the language, covering everything from basic syntax to advanced patterns that require conceptual shifts when moving from dynamically-typed languages like Python to statically-typed systems languages with compile-time memory safety. The book includes self-study format chapters that map closely to Python concepts you already know, introducing Rust-specific ideas like ownership and traits in Part II and advanced topics and migration in Part III.
summary::Mbodi AI (YC P25) Is Hiring Fou: Mbodi AI is building an embodied AI platform that enables robots to learn and operate like humans through natural language interactions. The company is backed by top investors and works with global industrial partners, including ABB and Fortune 100 customers. As a founding team member, you'll be responsible for developing and deploying cutting-edge machine learning models and agentic AI systems that power robot learning and behavior. This role requires strong experience in AI, robotics, machine learning, or agentic systems, as well as proficiency in Python and PyTorch, with experience training, evaluating, and deploying neural networks.
summary::If LLMs Have Human-Like Attribu: LLMs have been found to exhibit human-like attributes, including contextual understanding and emotional intelligence. This has significant implications for the development of more sophisticated AI systems that can interact with humans in a more natural way. As researchers continue to study these findings, we can expect to see new applications and innovations emerge.
summary::Life is too short for a slow te: The author is lamenting the slow performance of their terminal experience due to the use of a minimalistic shell with a focus on speed. They are highlighting how the lack of frameworks such as oh-my-zsh, prezto, or plugin managers can lead to unnecessary overhead and inefficiencies in managing plugins and themes. The author also notes that caching completions is one of the most expensive things in a typical .zshrc file, and lazy-loading nvm is another culprit that adds extra startup time.
summary::Nvidia is proposing a beast of: Nvidia is proposing a new high-performance CPU system designed specifically for Windows PCs. The system, codenamed 'Ampere', would feature a custom-designed 8-core and 16-thread processor with integrated graphics capabilities. This could significantly improve gaming performance and power efficiency compared to current systems. Nvidia's goal is to create a more powerful and affordable option for PC gamers and content creators.
summary::The new bibliomaniacs: Rare book collecting is booming as young people seek tangible connections to the past, with a man searching for a book outside a shop in Cecil Court. A group of fifty American booksellers formed the Antiquarian Booksellers' Association of America (ABAA) in 1949, and their first meeting was held at Parke-Bernet Galleries now Sotheby's. The association has grown to include over 174 exhibitors from around the world, with a value of the global rare book market exceeding $7 billion and expected growth above 6 per cent per year.
summary::Pokemon Emerald Ported to WebAs: Pokémon Emerald has been ported to the webassembly platform, allowing developers to run it on their computers with high performance and speed. The game's native code is being compiled into WebAssembly, a binary format that can be executed directly in modern browsers. This means users can enjoy fast-paced gameplay without the need for emulators or virtual machines. As a result, developers can focus on optimizing the game's performance rather than dealing with compatibility issues.
summary::Do women’s mate preferences cha: The study published in 2014 found that women's mate preferences do change across the ovulatory cycle, with some research suggesting a peak in attraction during the luteal phase and another peak during the follicular phase. This suggests that men may be more attracted to women who are fertile, which could have implications for fertility treatments and reproductive health.
summary::Benchmarks in Leipzig: A group of mathematicians compiled a dataset of research-level mathematics questions with known answers between April 1 and May 15, 2026, at the Max Planck Institute for Mathematics in Leipzig, Germany. The dataset consists of 100 questions that were evaluated using three stages: single attempts by five state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs), a 20-runs-per-model evaluation with three LLMs, and a 3-run attempt with two heavy-thinking models. This demonstrates the impressive mathematical reasoning capabilities of LLMs, which have become increasingly effective in solving complex problems.
summary::Building Rust Procedural Macros: Rust's procedural macros allow developers to manipulate the initial code in a higher-level way, making it easier to reason about and implement complex logic at various stages of program development. By using tokenization as the foundation for their macro implementation, developers can create reusable and modular code that is more efficient and maintainable than traditional programming approaches.
summary::Azure Linux Desktop: Azure Linux Desktop is a small Windows app that boots a full Linux desktop in a window, using the Azure Linux 4.0 base image and an embedded Linux container runtime provided by WSLc. The app uses Microsoft's UI Reactor framework to build its interface, which includes a title bar, boot page, and desktop surface. This project is notable for its use of GitHub repositories from Fedora Linux 43, which allows it to pull XFCE packages into the Azure Linux environment.
summary::Did Claude Increase Bugs in rsy: The author of the article is investigating whether Claude increased bugs in the rsync package after he became its maintainer. The author used AI assistance to avoid potential accusations of "AI slop" or hallucinations, and instead relied on their wife's expertise in statistics to develop a methodology for analyzing the data. The author spent several days rewriting the report using GLM 5.1 and Python scripts that automatically generated numbers and graphs based on the analysis.
summary::Moving beyond fork() + exec(): The core topic of this article is moving beyond the traditional fork() + exec() system call model, which can be expensive and inefficient due to the need to copy process state and memory. Li Chen's proposal for spawn templates aims to optimize this pattern by creating a template for an executable file that can be reused across multiple invocations, reducing the overhead of copying and discarding memory.
summary::Tribute to Jiro Yamada, Automot: A tribute to Jiro Yamada, an automotive artist who passed away in 2025, has been released as a video. The article discusses the impact of his work on the automotive industry and its influence on developers. His artistic style has inspired many designers and developers, and his legacy will continue to be felt through his innovative designs.
summary::US House lawmakers release draf: A new draft bill has been released by US House lawmakers aimed at preventing states from imposing their own regulations on artificial intelligence. The proposed legislation would require federal agencies to follow existing international standards when developing and deploying AI systems, rather than allowing individual states to dictate how the technology is used. This move could have significant implications for industries such as healthcare and finance that rely heavily on AI. By prohibiting state-level AI rules, lawmakers hope to create a more level playing field for companies operating in multiple jurisdictions.
summary::Ergonomic overrides for Nixpkgs: Ergonomic overrides for Nixpkgs are a new package that simplifies the process of overriding dependencies in Nixpkgs, which is part of a broader effort to improve usability. This project aims to address the fundamental problem of type-checking Nix, where nobody actually uses it at any significant scale, by creating embedded languages within Nix for programming "in the large". By designing better abstractions, such as override functions and overlays, developers can build more user-friendly tools that work well in inexpert hands.
summary::Police in England and Wales tol: Police forces in England and Wales are being advised by a government agency not to use artificial intelligence (AI) in their court statements. The decision comes after concerns were raised about the potential for AI to manipulate or distort testimony, which could undermine the integrity of the justice system. The advice is part of a broader effort to ensure that technology used in courts is transparent and accountable.
summary::Running Python code in a sandbo: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM allows developers to execute arbitrary code safely within their own applications, meeting the need for plugins that extend software features without risking core application security or user data exposure. This approach enables the creation of mechanisms such as scheduled code execution, data transformation, and database updates, all while adhering to memory and CPU limits. By using MicroPython in WebAssembly, developers can build sandboxed environments that are easy to install, secure, and controlled, making it an attractive solution for various use cases.
summary::You Can Run: When two young sisters were taken from their privileged lives and given fake names by their parents, they thought it was because of a business issue, but years later they learned the truth about their father's life of crime. By then, Erin McCann had already left Philadelphia to escape her mother's secrets, but she still wanted answers. She finally got the chance when she drove past farms in Pennsylvania Dutch country and overheard her mother's voice telling her that two boxes filled with government documents were at the curb with the trash.
summary::URL Parser Tester: URL parser tester allows developers to compare outputs of various parsers on a given URL, supporting multiple programming languages and browsers. The test makes heavy use of WebAssembly and Web Workers for compatibility with Safari, Firefox, and Chromium-based browsers. A list of URLs that have diverged in the past has been compiled, including those using IDNA 2003 vs 2008 and Go net/url vs Node.js legacy.
summary::Trees to Flows and Back: Unifyi: Decision trees and diffusion models are being unified by establishing a mathematical correspondence between hierarchical decision trees and diffusion processes in limiting regimes. This unification reveals a shared optimization principle known as Global Trajectory Score Matching or GTSM, which is asymptotically optimal for gradient boosting in idealized versions. The work has practical instantiations such as treeflow, a method that achieves competitive generation quality on tabular data with higher fidelity and a computational speedup, and dsmtree, a novel distillation method that transfers hierarchical decision logic into neural networks matching teacher performance within 2% on many benchmarks.
summary::Zeroserve: A zero-config web se: Zeroserve is a zero-config web server that can be scripted with eBPF, offering fast performance and efficient scripting capabilities. It allows developers to create a declarative configuration language for routing, headers, authentication, rate limiting, and proxying, while also providing an optional scripting runtime bolted on the side. This enables a single, ordinary program to see every request and decide what happens, resulting in a compact and deployable solution with no config file required.
summary::The smallest C++ binary: The smallest C++ binary is a program that runs successfully with no errors and produces a deterministically zero exit status. It must be created using the GCC compiler, stripped of debugging information, and produced by only linking against the standard library. The resulting binary can be as small as 13632 bytes, but it still includes some code that is not executed before entering the main function.
summary::Astronomers measure the mass of: Astronomers have used the James Webb Space Telescope to directly measure the mass of a dormant black hole 10 billion light-years away. This achievement allows researchers to peer inside the black hole's sphere of influence and gain insights into its formation billions of years ago. By combining data from JWST with gravitational lensing, scientists can weigh black holes more accurately than ever before.
summary::Ntsc-rs – open-source video emu: Ntsc-rs is an open-source video effect that accurately emulates analog TV and VHS artifacts by using algorithms to model the actual transmission and encoding processes of these formats. Unlike similar effects such as ntscQT, it can run in real time at much higher resolutions than actual NTSC footage. Developed in Rust and multithreaded for SIMD acceleration, ntsc-rs is available as a standalone application, web app, or plugin for various video editing software.
summary::Meta confirms 1000s of Instagra: Meta has confirmed that thousands of Instagram accounts were hacked by abusing its AI chatbot, which hackers repeatedly tricked into taking control of a person's account. The company notified at least 20,225 people whose accounts were compromised, including 30 in Maine, due to a vulnerability in an AI-assisted account recovery system for Instagram. This breach relates to the bug that allowed anyone to reset password resets on user accounts without verifying the email address associated with the account.
summary::Pentagon raised threat of Israe: The Pentagon has raised its threat of Israel spying on the US to its highest level, citing concerns that Israel is making a concerted effort to surveil top US officials for sensitive information about the administration's Middle East policies. This move was prompted by recent incidents where Israel allegedly attempted to gather intelligence on US government officials, including President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. The Pentagon has issued an internal message indicating that Israel's ability to conduct human espionage and technical collection is at a "critical level", which is the highest threat level assigned by the Defense Intelligence Agency.
summary::Getting silly with C, part &((i: Researchers have discovered a potential vulnerability in the C programming language that could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected systems. The issue is related to the use of a pointer arithmetic operation involving an integer and a negative value. This type of attack can be particularly damaging because it doesn't require the attacker to know the exact location of sensitive data, making it harder to defend against. Developers who write C code should take note of this finding to ensure their applications are secure.
summary::A Little Explanation of Little': A simple idea from computer science can be applied to many real-world scenarios where queues are involved. Little's Law states that if you have enough resources (such as coffee machines or employees), the average time customers spend waiting for service is directly proportional to the rate at which they arrive and inversely proportional to the length of the line. This means that increasing the throughput by adding more resources can help reduce wait times, making it a useful concept in many fields beyond just coffee shops.
summary::Universal Memory Protocol – a s: Universal Memory Protocol is a transport-neutral memory protocol for AI agents that enables the sharing of agent memory across sessions, agents, and vendors. It standardizes how agents remember portable knowledge, making it easier to extend existing memories without starting over. This technology matters to developers as it addresses a long-standing problem in AI development where agents struggle to share and manage their own memories. By providing a standardized way to store and retrieve agent memory, UMP enables new agents and stores to build upon existing knowledge, facilitating more efficient and effective AI systems.
summary::Home alone: Remote work, isolat: Remote work has become increasingly popular in recent years, but a growing body of research suggests that excessive isolation can have negative effects on both physical and mental health. The lack of face-to-face interaction with colleagues and friends can lead to feelings of loneliness and disconnection. As remote workers spend more time alone at home, they may be more likely to experience anxiety, depression, and other mental health issues. Understanding the impact of isolation on mental health is essential for developers who work remotely or have family members who do.
summary::Computex 2026: Are We Heading f: The upcoming Computex 2026 is expected to showcase significant advancements in artificial intelligence and machine learning, with many exhibitors showcasing demos of AI-powered devices. Researchers are exploring ways to integrate AI into various industries such as healthcare, finance, and transportation. The potential applications of these technologies could lead to more efficient and personalized solutions for developers working on projects that rely heavily on AI. As the field continues to evolve, it's likely that we'll see significant changes in how we design and build software.
summary::New U.S. college grads now have: New college graduates now face higher unemployment than the average worker, with recent graduates having an unemployment rate of around 5.6%, compared to 4.2% for all workers. This reversal started in early 2019 and has continued unabated since then, despite a healthy overall unemployment rate of 4.2%. The gap is widest on record, with about 41% of recent graduates underemployed while working at jobs that do not match their skills or experience.
summary::Computex 2026: Are We Heading f: The keynote speakers at Computex 2026 are expected to discuss the future of personal computers, with a focus on artificial intelligence and machine learning. The event is also likely to feature announcements about new hardware and software technologies that could shape the development of agnostic PCs. As AI and ML continue to advance, developers will need to consider how these technologies can be integrated into their projects. This trend may have significant implications for the future of computing.
summary::Sem: New primitive for code und: Semantic understanding on top of Git has been enhanced with a new primitive called sem, which provides entity-level diffs and rename detection, structural hashing, and word-level inline highlights. This technology allows developers to analyze code changes in a more accurate and efficient manner, making it easier to identify issues and understand the impact of changes on the system.
summary::What Columbus used instead of t: Mariners sailing for Spain and Portugal in the 1490s did not use the North Star as true north due to its location being two thirds of a degree away from the North Celestial Pole. This discrepancy means that if you were aiming for Miami, Florida, you could wind up in the Florida Keys instead. The Earth's axis wobbles around relative to the sky, taking about 26,000 years for one complete wobble, and this is called axial precession.
summary::Harness engineering: Leveraging: Codex, a new AI-powered platform from Microsoft, is revolutionizing the way developers interact with their code. By leveraging codex's agent-first approach, developers can automate tasks, improve collaboration, and enhance productivity. As the developer community continues to shift towards more agile and flexible development methodologies, codex's capabilities will play a crucial role in driving innovation. Its integration with Azure DevOps and GitHub will further accelerate the adoption of this technology.
summary::Public Domain Image Archive: The Public Domain Image Archive offers a vast collection of 11,082 out-of-copyright works that can be freely browsed, downloaded, and reused. The archive is constantly updated with new images added every week, making it a valuable resource for developers looking to incorporate public domain assets into their projects. By leveraging this database, developers can tap into a rich source of free and open-source images, reducing their reliance on copyrighted materials and increasing their creative freedom.
summary::Google to pay SpaceX $920M a mo: Google is paying SpaceX $920 million per month for compute capacity at its xAI data centers. This deal is part of Google's efforts to expand its artificial intelligence capabilities and accelerate research in the field. The company plans to use the resources provided by SpaceX to support its machine learning projects, including those related to autonomous vehicles and other applications.
summary::Show HN: Keybench – Scriptable,: Keybench is a scriptable performance tool for sorted key value stores that allows developers to measure throughput and latency by writing workloads in Lua and running them across multiple storage engines, providing two rates (view cart or checkout) and a distribution of latency per operation kind. This tool can be used to compare the performance of different storage engines and harnesses, making it an extensible and customizable solution for optimizing key value store performance.
summary::An Ohio Valley 100k-Watt FM Sig: A 100,000-watt FM signal was severed in broad daylight by a copper thief cutting its wires, leaving the broadcast tower of country-formatted WDGG(FM) without power. The alleged perpetrator, Paul Crisp of Catlettsburg, allegedly dragged the heavy line down to his house and cut it into smaller sections before escaping. The Boyd County Sheriff's Office obtained a search warrant and charged Crisp with second-degree fleeing or evading police after a short pursuit.
summary::Tokenomics: Quantifying Where T: Tokenomics, a field that studies the quantification of tokens in software development, has been applied to analyze the operational efficiency and resource consumption of Large Language Models (LLMs) in automated software engineering tasks. The study examines token distribution across distinct stages of the Software Development Life Cycle, revealing that the iterative Code Review stage accounts for the majority of token consumption, with input tokens constituting the largest share. This finding suggests that the primary cost of agentic software engineering lies not in initial code generation but in automated refinement and verification.
summary::How LLMs Actually Work: Modern language models like transformers rely on a complex set of algorithms and techniques, including tokenization, embeddings, positional encoding, attention, the feed-forward network, residual streams, and layer normalization. These components work together to predict the next token in a sequence, allowing the model to generate coherent text. The architecture of these models is shared across many different variants, but their training data and configuration choices can significantly impact their performance.
summary::Show HN: DomainTasker – avoid l: DomainTasker is an automated domain renewal service that helps users avoid losing their domains due to surprise renewals. The tool analyzes a user's domain registration history and sends reminders when renewal dates are approaching, allowing them to take action before the domains expire. By streamlining the process of managing domain registrations, DomainTasker aims to save developers time and reduce errors. Its core technology is a robust web application built using modern JavaScript frameworks such as React and Node.js.
summary::Unicode Fonts and Tools for X11: Unicode fonts and tools for X11 are now available, extending all "misc-fixed-*" fonts to include characters from ISO 8859 parts 1-5, 7-10, 13-15, as well as the CEN MES-1 European Unicode Subset, IBM/Microsoft code pages, Microsoft/Adobe Windows Glyph List 4, KOI8-R, and DEC VT100 graphics symbols.
summary::How Liminalism Became the Defin: Giorgio de Chirico's "The Red Tower" (1913) has become a defining aesthetic of our time, evoking feelings of disquiet and nostalgia in those who experience it. This eerie image, captured by Dave Columbus on Facebook, exemplifies the popular internet aesthetic of liminality, where past, present, and future collide. The abandoned Century III Mall in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania is a prime example of this phenomenon, as its empty spaces and vacant stores create a sense of melancholic discomfort that resonates with contemporary artists and internet users alike.
summary::Field of clones: How horse repl: Horse replicas have become increasingly popular in the world of polo, with many players opting for artificial mounts instead of live horses. This trend is driven by a desire for consistency and reliability on the field, as well as concerns about animal welfare. The use of clones has also led to significant cost savings for teams, making it more accessible to smaller clubs or those without access to high-quality live horses. As a result, polo players are increasingly turning to horse replicas to compete at the highest level.
summary::Show HN: Oproxy – inspect and m: Oproxy is a local HTTP, HTTPS, and SOCKS5 proxy for inspecting, replaying, and modifying network traffic. It allows developers to test browsers, CLIs, mobile apps, API clients, services, and test suites on their own machine or in a local Docker container. The included Compose file uses host networking, persists certificates and storage, and sets up OpenAI-compatible chat model for authenticated Assistant inspection and proxy changes.
summary::Valve P2P networking broken for: Valve's P2P networking system has been experiencing major issues in Israel and possibly other Middle Eastern countries, causing problems with online gaming experiences for players. The problem affects games such as Street Fighter 6, where ping times between players are significantly higher than expected. This issue is not exclusive to Israeli players and may be region-wide due to the widespread nature of the problem.
summary::DIY Kobo annotation backup: The DIY Kobo annotation backup script allows developers to create a PDF document containing annotations from borrowed ebooks, using the data stored in the Kobo library. The script uses SQLite and ImageMagick to extract page screenshots and combine them into a single image file, which can then be used for referencing purposes.
summary::Human-Like Neural Nets by Catap: Human-Like Neural Nets by Catapulting propose to create artificial neural nets with human-like performance through high-learning-rate/regularization training of overparameterized NNs, which would resolve many outstanding mysteries of artificial versus natural intelligence. This approach could lead to sample-efficiently and compute-efficiently traveling to a highly-generalizing human-like basin in the model loss landscape, while performing poorly up until the end and failing to memorize much data.
summary::Symbolica 2.0: Programmable Sym: Symbolica 2.0 introduces programmable symbols, allowing users to customize the behavior of mathematical objects in Python and Rust. This enables more flexibility and control over symbolic computations, making it possible to define complex mathematical functions that simplify, differentiate, expand, print, and evaluate like built-ins. The new features include a simpler Rust API with operator overloading, symbol registration systems, and improved evaluator interfaces, resulting in better output for notebooks and documents.
summary::The 29th International Obfuscat: The 29th International Obfuscated C Code Contest (IOCCC) 2025 winners were selected through a combination of quality submissions and improved website design, leading to a higher-than-usual submission quality despite a similar number of entries as last year. The contest's procedures for closing new submissions, judging, and updating the website have been documented, resulting in overall improvements to how the IOCCC is run. This year's winners will be updated with links to YouTube segments under their index.html pages, making it easier for developers to learn from and build upon past winning entries.
summary::I design with Claude more than: I design with Claude more than Figma now, where I've found that artificial intelligence support has become indispensable. With LLMs like Copilot and Cursor, I can generate changes to my game without having to rely on them, which was a major disappointment in the past. Now, I use AI to build prototype features that are exactly what I need, saving me time and effort. This workflow improvement is particularly welcome since I've been working with OCaml and Bonsai, two new technologies that require significant expertise.
summary::The Guix Nix Abomination: Lever: Guix and Nix are two ecosystems that share similarities but also have distinct differences. Guix derivations leverage Guix derivations in Nix, allowing for hermetic builds by declaring all necessary resources upfront. This approach ensures that the build process only finds resources that have been declared explicitly.
summary::Speculative KV coding: lossless: Speculative KV coding is a method for losslessly compressing the KV cache of a large target model by up to ~4× using a predictor model that runs in parallel on both encode and decode sides. This approach uses an arithmetic coder to encode the true cache at a bitrate set by how well the predictor fits the target, resulting in a compression ratio of ~8×.
summary::To my students: The software industry is facing a crisis of values, with many companies prioritizing short-term profits over long-term sustainability and intellectual property respect. As computer scientists, it's essential to be aware of these issues and make deliberate choices about how we work and what technologies we use. By cultivating our ability to think deeply and prioritize ethics, developers can carve out distraction-free bubbles for themselves and others, and strive to create positive change in the industry.
summary::Scientists ejected from diabete: Scientists who handed out copies of an editorial criticizing the Trump administration's attacks on scientific research at an American Diabetes Association meeting have been ousted from the event. The five scientists, including a professor and editor-in-chief of a prominent medical journal, were removed after allegedly violating the organization's code of conduct for conferences by distributing reprints of their published work outside a scheduled speaking engagement.
summary::Efficient and Training-Free Sin: Computer Science Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition arXiv:2606.04299 Efficient and Training-Free Single-Image Diffusion Models Authors Haojun Qiu Kiriakos N Kutulakos David B Lindell This article considers the problem of generating images whose internal structure matches that of a single reference image, using a dataset of patches at different scales to model the image. A training-free approach is proposed by modeling the image using this dataset and integrating an optimal denoiser into an efficient image diffusion model. The method achieves state-of-the-art generation quality and diversity compared to trained models, and demonstrates applications such as unconditional image generation and text-guided stylization.
summary::Exploring automatic Buffer Mana: Exploring automatic Buffer Management with io_uring provides a low-level API for performing I/O operations using io_uring, which allows for asynchronous I/O on recent Linux kernels. The feature enables harnessing the full power of io_uring to bring high-performance I/O to Ruby applications that support fiber concurrency. Automatic buffer management is a key technology in this implementation, allowing for efficient use of kernel buffers and improved performance in I/O operations.
summary::9 Mothers (YC P26) Is Hiring: The company 9 Mothers is hiring for various roles in their counter-drone systems development. The work involves building hardware and software components with a focus on autonomous perception, kinetic engagement, and the kill chain that closes drones. This team is looking for individuals who can own the design and build systems from scratch, with a emphasis on short iteration loops and real deployment. They are seeking engineers in machine learning, computer vision, robotics, electrical engineering, mechatronics, and R&D test engineering to join their team.

summary::What is the purpose of the lost: The lost+found folder in Linux and Unix operating systems is a directory used to store incomplete or deleted files. summary::Config Files That Run Code: Sup: Config files that run code are often overlooked in supply chain security assessments due to their innocuous nature, but they can be exploited by attackers to inject malicious commands into a developer's workflow. summary::Premature Optimization is Fun S: A connectivity monitoring system is being developed that sends ICMP Echo Requests to multiple servers and monitors latency and dropped packet averages over different time periods. A more efficient solution is proposed by using a tagged union, which stores the relevant information in a single field, reducing memory usage.
summary::Show HN: Performative-UI – a re: The core topic of this article is a React component library called Performative-UI, which aims to provide a set of reusable UI components that mimic common design patterns. The key technology used in the library is React, allowing developers to leverage its vast ecosystem and tools for building complex applications. By incorporating these design tropes into their codebase, developers can streamline their workflow and create more cohesive user interfaces.
summary::HTMX Is So Cool I Rolled My Own: HTMX rejects modern JavaScript UI in favour of server-rendered HTML, building on old ideas like infinite scroll and live search results. Popular examples include infinite scroll and live search results, which are often implemented using React or other front-end frameworks. HTMX is a replacement for front-end Svelte, but not a "magic bullet" that solves all problems, and its limitations may be more important than the technology itself.
summary::Are you expected to run five Py: Mypy, Pyrefly, and Pyright are type-checkers that library maintainers must run on their test suite to ensure accurate type checking. Running these tools on internal source code is not sufficient as it tests internal logic rather than the public API. By running multiple type-checkers over a test suite, developers can guarantee that their package's public API works well for many users.
summary::I wish Deno would keep doing wh: The author is lamenting that Deno's shift towards Node.js compatibility and features such as specifier support and npm package management are diluting its unique value proposition. The author notes that Deno's decision to add these features was likely driven by the need to catch up with Node.js, rather than a desire to improve TypeScript itself. This shift is making Deno feel less like a specialized tool for Haskell or other languages, but more like an attempt to emulate Node.js.
summary::LLMs and performative productiv: LLMs and performative productivity began to make me wonder exactly how productive AI is making me personally, as I stepped back from using agents for day-to-day tasks. I found that my newfound capabilities allowed me to accomplish more, faster, and with less effort than before, but also led to a lack of understanding about the codebases and user-side changes. The new features I built were neat, but not actually being used, and the greenfield projects quickly abandoned. I questioned whether it was all a net gain, considering I gained little real context or value from my work.
summary::Indie game Dogpile is coming to: Dogpile, an indie game about merging dogs into larger ones, will be released on Nintendo Switch and mobile devices. The game features 13 new doggos with two new traits: zoomy and stinky, as well as a challenge mode for added difficulty. Dogpile has already gained popularity on Steam, where it was initially launched last year, and is now being rolled out to other platforms in the coming weeks.
summary::I replaced Spotify with a homem: A developer is using Python and the `pydub` library to create a custom FM radio station that can replace Spotify. The project involves creating an audio player that uses the `pygame` library to play pre-recorded audio files. By leveraging these technologies, developers can build their own media players without relying on third-party services like Spotify. This approach allows for greater flexibility and control over the user experience.
summary::MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed: 1T mo: Xiaomi MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed is a 1T model that can decode 1000 tokens per second, breaking the previous record for this parameter and offering a paradigm shift in AI application speed. This achievement enables real-time generation of models at speeds previously unimaginable, revolutionizing how intelligence is defined and harnessed.
summary::Why are so many young people ge: Researchers are investigating why young people are developing cancers at an alarming rate, with over 9,000 cases diagnosed daily worldwide under the age of 50. A landmark trial has shown success against "undruggable" cancer, but it is unclear what causes these cancers in this demographic. The increasing incidence of certain cancers among young adults may be due to changes in how they are detected or classified, such as a shift towards early-onset pancreatic cancer diagnoses after a change in the definition of that condition.
summary::A Farmer Donated Land to Turn i: A farmer donated 87 acres of land in Texas to a small city, which sold it to a data center developer for $10 million. The land is now being used to build a massive data center, with the potential impact on local residents and the environment considered. This development highlights the tension between economic growth and community interests.
summary::Release Fil-C Linux/x86_64 vers: The developers of the Fil-C Linux/x86_64 version 0.679 release have made several key improvements, including the inclusion of rsync and its dependencies, OpenSSH rebased to 10.3p1 with post-quantum crypto support, and improved performance by introducing a new calling convention. These changes make the /opt/fil distribution even more useful than before, while also addressing various bugs and issues.
summary::Full Reverse Engineering of the: The TI-84 Plus Operating System is being reverse-engineered using Ghidra, a tool that disassembles and deciphers the operating system's code. The project aims to understand how the OS manages memory, paging, and system calls, which are crucial for understanding its behavior. By analyzing the OS's architecture and implementation details, developers can gain insights into the underlying mechanisms that govern the TI-84 Plus's functionality.
summary::Siri AI: Apple is introducing a new AI-powered assistant called Siri AI that will be available later this year. This new version of Siri will be centered around the user's needs and context, providing more helpful and personalized interactions. It will integrate into various apps, including Photos, Messages, and Safari, and offer advanced features such as Visual Intelligence for search and action capabilities, Spatial Reframing for photo editing, and Extended and Clean Up tools for photo management.
summary::Apple WWDC 2026 Livestream: Apple is introducing a new Siri AI-powered feature that promises to make interactions with the virtual assistant more natural and helpful, including richer answers and conversational capabilities. The update also includes expanded child safety features and performance enhancements across various devices. These changes are part of a broader effort by Apple to improve user experience and provide more intuitive interfaces for its products.
summary::Thunderbird Littering My Home: The developer community is likely to find the issue with Thunderbird's directory creation bug in recent XDG changes useful for automating directory management and improving home directory organization. This problem highlights the importance of understanding how different technologies interact, such as how watch-thunderbird-dir.fish can be used to monitor and manage directories.
summary::A programming language for huma: A statically typed functional programming language called EYG has been developed as a better alternative to existing languages for end users who want to create software without needing extensive programming knowledge. This language aims to simplify the process by providing a visual interface and eliminating the need for developers to manually handle complex logic. By focusing on problem-solving rather than building complex systems, EYG seeks to cater to individuals who prefer "home cooked" solutions over mass-produced software.
summary::Stop the Apple Music app from l: The Apple Music app appears to be launching even when the system music app is running, causing issues with keyboard control. This happens because the Music Decoy app has a bundle identifier of com.apple.Music and makes the system think it's already running by sharing this identifier. To disable this, developers can configure the Music Decoy app to launch another app when the Play button is pressed or reset its configuration. The issue is caused by the Remote Control Daemon (rcd), which checks for playing audio apps before launching the system music app. Developers can use tools like launchctl to unload the rcd plist file or alternative methods such as quitting the Music Decoy app manually, but these solutions have their own limitations and drawbacks.
summary::Vulnerability and malware check: Vulnerability and malware checks in uv are two new security features announced by the project. The first feature, uv audit, scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and "adverse" project statuses, with improved performance compared to existing tools like pip-audit. The second feature allows for OSV-based malware lookup on every sync operation, providing a lightweight alternative to previous methods. Both are in preview and considered unstable, but developers can try them out and share feedback.
summary::xAI is looking more like a data: xAI's partnerships with Anthropic and Google are providing them with massive capacity, but it's worth considering that this deal may not be as lucrative for the company as initially thought due to cancellation clauses and potential red flags. The partnership allows xAI to access older datacentre infrastructure in Memphis, reversing usage limit restrictions on subscriptions, which has helped alleviate peak time crunch issues. However, the enormous fees involved - $1.25bn/month for 300MW of capacity - raise concerns about the sustainability of this deal.
summary::Mutation Testing in Haskell: Mutation testing aims to improve test suites by automatically mutating code and asserting that the tests start failing, providing a more thorough assessment of code quality than traditional testing methods. This technology can be particularly useful for developers who have noticed a decrease in confidence in their own code's quality due to the rapid production of complex software systems.
summary::Why are cells small?: Cells come in a wide range of shapes and sizes, with the smallest human cell being just 30 µm³ while the largest oocyte is over 4 million µm³, spanning five orders of magnitude. The size difference can be attributed to evolution's need for each cell type to have the optimal volume for its function, but physics also plays a significant role in determining cell size limits. The surface area-to-volume ratio and diffusion rates dictate how quickly molecules can move within cells, with larger molecules diffusing slower than smaller ones due to their increased interaction with obstacles and other molecules.
summary::AI Is Slowing Down: AI Is Slowing Down Ed Zitron Jun 8, 2026 24 min read If you liked this piece, you should subscribe to my premium newsletter for a weekly newsletter usually around 5,000 to 18,000 words. summary::EU-banned pesticides found in r: EU-banned pesticides have been found in a wide range of everyday food products including rice, tea, and spices. Laboratory tests on 64 products from four European countries detected residues of multiple pesticides, many of which are no longer approved for use in the EU. These chemicals can still be exported to third countries and then imported back into Europe as residues, posing a risk to consumers.
summary::Apple reveals new AI architectu: Apple has unveiled a new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models, which are adapted to run both on-device and on servers through its existing Private Cloud Compute infrastructure. The new architecture centers on Apple Foundation Models co-developed with Google, unlocking state-of-the-art understanding and reasoning capabilities as well as multimodal support including image understanding and generation. summary::Apple Core AI Framework: Apple has released a new core AI framework that enables developers to build more sophisticated machine learning models. The framework, which is available for use with iOS 14 and macOS Big Sur, provides a set of tools and APIs for tasks such as image classification, object detection, and natural language processing. summary::Forgejo monthly report - May 20: The Forgejo monthly report highlights the release of v15.0.3, which includes security fixes and a new entrypoint customization feature for job containers and services. summary::Ask HN: Why hasn&#x27;t there b: Ticketmaster's dominance in the ticketing market seems puzzling given the competition from other platforms, but it appears to be a result of its strategic partnerships and ownership by the same company. summary::This Month in Redox - May 2026: Redox OS has made significant progress in May with the implementation of the EEVDF scheduler, boasting ~200 FPS gain in Pixelcannon and improved timeout accuracy on both function and real hardware. The new scheduler features more dynamic calculations than its predecessor, ensuring tasks are given enough CPU time even when doing many system calls or having lower process priority. summary::I&#x27;m building a parallel in: Building a parallel internet called The Thinnernet involves creating a separate network for data transmission that can handle massive amounts of data without being limited by current internet speeds. This concept is based on the idea that the user experience will be shaped more by infrastructure and social networks than hardware, with many people relying on these networks voluntarily to facilitate seamless interactions.
summary::Ask HN: What are tools you have: Hacker News users are sharing their tools they've created since the advent of AI, from self-hosting LLM-wiki systems to TUI tools for organizing and authoring sermons and yek models for project estimates. summary::Show HN: Performative-UI – A re: The development of a React component library focused on showcasing and utilizing common UI design patterns has been announced. summary::OpenAI Submits S-1 Draft to SEC: OpenAI has submitted its initial public filing, or S-1 draft, with the US Securities and Exchange Commission. As a leading provider of conversational AI solutions, OpenAI's technology enables businesses to interact with customers in more natural and intuitive ways. summary::FrontierCode: Menu Close Raising the bar from correctness to quality models can write correct code, but as AI-generated code becomes dominant in production, the question is whether they can also write good code. FrontierCode measures how well models meet high-quality production codebase standards by assessing end-to-end code quality through novel ensemble of grading techniques and rigorous quality control. The benchmark employs a subjective rubric grading system with adversarial testing and manual review by Cognition researchers to achieve an 81% lower false positive rate compared to SWE-Bench Pro, providing the strongest available signal of a model's ability to write high-quality code.
summary::Surveillance Is Not Safety: A s: The UK's latest threat to privacy is a surveillance statement that has sparked concerns among developers and tech experts. The document, which was leaked in PDF format, details the government's plans to expand its surveillance powers under the Investigatory Powers Act (IPA). According to reports, the UK is considering introducing new measures to allow for mass surveillance of citizens' online activities, including social media and messaging apps. This move has raised concerns about the potential erosion of civil liberties and the impact on personal data protection.
summary::Show HN: Gitdot – a better GitH: Gitdot is an open-source alternative to GitHub, written in Rust. It aims to simplify the process of managing and sharing code. The project's core feature is its use of a graph database to store repository metadata, allowing for more efficient searching and filtering of repositories. This approach could improve developer productivity by reducing the time spent on tedious tasks like searching for specific files or users.
summary::Self-hosting email the hard way: Running your own email infrastructure can be a valuable learning experience for developers, especially those new to systems and networking. By installing OpenBSD and fixing bugs in PHP, the author has gained hands-on knowledge of how the Internet works. Self-hosting is also important for sovereign access to one's own data as the web consolidates among few players, with two companies able to mostly read email traffic due to MX diversity.
summary::Introduction to nixidy - Kubern: nixidy is an extension to Kubernetes GitOps that replaces Helm value files, Kustomize overlays, and raw YAML with typed Nix expressions per environment, allowing for more reproducible builds and easier debugging. By using a single Nix expression per environment, every field in the deployment is typed, making it easy to review and deploy changes.
summary::What I got wrong about fast ter: A stripped-down config isn't the only way to achieve fast shells, and a modern, static-bundling manager can manage updates for you, making it slower than a framework that resolves plugins on every startup. The author's measurement of total init time plus teardown was misleading, focusing on the wrong aspect of the benchmark. Instant prompt renders a cached prompt before .zshrc finishes loading, rendering the exit-time number irrelevant in practice.
summary::Federal judge blocks H1B visa $: A federal judge ruled that President Donald Trump's policy imposing a $100,000 fee for new H1B visas is unconstitutional and invalid. The fee was part of a broader policy aimed at increasing revenue from the program, but it was not explained by the administration and found to be a tax. summary::We Think the SpaceX IPO Is Over: The recent SpaceX IPO has sparked debate among investors and analysts about its valuation. The company's valuation has been criticized as being overly high due to its strong growth prospects and innovative technology. One key area of concern is the potential for increased competition in the satellite launch market, which could impact SpaceX's pricing power and profitability. summary::Cleaning up after AI rockstar d: Cleaning up after AI rockstar developers Photo by Martijn Baudoin on Unsplash We've all worked with a rockstar developer who joined years ago, full of energy and great ideas about new tech and architectures. Their cutting-edge ideas left everyone else feeling behind and outdated as they rewrote the company's core architecture and introduced new build processes. The hardest tasks were assigned to them, and their code was often incomprehensible, leading to a culture where nobody understood it but no one would admit it. After they left, teams struggled to keep up with their projects, dealing with messy codebases that required weeks to fix and sometimes even years to rewrite.
summary::Job: Head of Stonehenge: The Head of Stonehenge position at English Heritage is a permanent role that involves leading a large team to deliver exceptional visitor experiences while driving strong financial performance. summary::GoGoGrandparent (YC S16) is hir: GoGoGrandparent is hiring a Senior Backend Engineer with expertise in Node.js and TypeScript to design build and maintain scalable backend systems. This role involves delivering high-quality features end-to-end, collaborating closely with product managers, and continuously improving the codebase through thoughtful refactoring. summary::Old&#x27;aVista – The most powe: Old'aVista is a powerful guide to the old Internet, providing access to archived websites and information from the early days of the web. This technology allows developers to explore and learn from the history of the internet, which matters for those who want to build and maintain modern online applications. summary::Porting the ThinkPad X61 to Cor: Porting the ThinkPad x61 to coreboot requires reverse-engineering the firmware, which involves analyzing and understanding the vendor's BIOS to identify its components and functionality.
